name: Buttermilk Lemon Pudding Cake with Blackberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. FOR SAUCE: Puree the berries with sugar, Grand Marnier, and lemon juice in a blender.
Strain the sauce through a fine sieve to remove seeds. Cover and refrigerate; can be prepared up to 1 day ahead.
2. FOR CAKE:
- Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and butter a 6- or 8-cup glass loaf dish.
- In a large bowl, blend together the sugar and flour. Mix in lemon juice, melted butter, lemon peel, and egg yolks until well combined.
- In a medium bowl, beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add the additional 1/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
- Gently fold the beaten egg white mixture into the batter.
- Pour the batter into the prepared loaf dish and place it in a large baking pan. Pour hot water into the baking pan until it reaches halfway up the sides of the loaf dish.
- Bake for about 1 hour and 10 minutes, or until the top is lightly golden.
- Remove the loaf dish from the water bath and let cool for at least 15 minutes.
3. Dust the cooled cake with powdered sugar. Spoon warm or hot cake into deep bowls.
4. Drizzle with sauce and garnish with berries. Serve any extra sauce separately.
